title: Louis XVI declaration before flight
citation: Louis XVI, Declaration adressee aux Francais, 20 June 1791, Archives nationales AE/II/1218.
limitations: Establishes Louis's stated rationale, not every private motive.

title: Jean-Baptiste Drouet report to the National Assembly
citation: Drouet, report delivered to the National Assembly, 24 June 1791, Archives parlementaires 27:508-509.
limitations: Retrospective participant report that may emphasize Drouet's own role.

title: Varennes municipal record
citation: Proces-verbal de ce qui s'est passe a Varennes, municipal record dated 27 June 1791.
limitations: Only partly independent of Drouet because he signed the municipal record.

title: Reviewed route and timing synthesis
citation: Timothy Tackett, When the King Took Flight, with the reviewed route records listed in the source ledger.
limitations: Bounds a schematic reconstruction and does not create a new eyewitness line.

title: National Assembly proclamation of 22 June 1791
citation: National Assembly proclamation, 22 June 1791, Musee Carnavalet AFF3437.
limitations: Official framing does not prove abduction, consensus, or coordinated deception.

title: Constitution of 3 September 1791
citation: French Constitution of 3 September 1791, Title III, article 4.
limitations: Establishes institutional continuity, not restored trust or agreement about the king.

title: William Short diplomatic observation
citation: William Short to Thomas Jefferson, 26 June 1791, Founders Online.
limitations: One diplomatic observer's report, not a national opinion survey.

title: Cordeliers petition
citation: Cordeliers petition of 14 July 1791 in Albert Mathiez, Le Club des Cordeliers pendant la crise de Varennes, pp. 112-115.
limitations: Radical club advocacy, not universal public opinion; keep separate from the 21 June petition.

title: Bodyguard judicial testimony
citation: Moustier and Maldent judicial testimony in Eugene Bimbenet, Fuite de Louis XVI a Varennes, 2nd ed.
limitations: Shared judicial dossier; counts as one preparation lineage, not two.

title: Fersen papers
citation: R. M. de Klinckowstrom, ed., Le comte de Fersen et la cour de France, vol. 1.
limitations: Family-edited participant papers with a damaged journal leaf and disclosed editorial uncertainty.

title: Reviewed novice context and constitutional chronology
citation: National Assembly chronology and reviewed constitutional context listed in the source ledger.
limitations: Provides novice orientation and does not count as eyewitness corroboration.

title: Departure of the king for Saint-Cloud print
citation: Anonymous, Depart du Roi pour St Cloud, Revolutions de Paris no. 93, p. 62, 1791, Musee Carnavalet G.28356.
limitations: Contemporary political print and caption supporting the prevented 18 April departure; it does not establish permanent imprisonment or nationwide opinion.
